title: Caspase 5c amplifies Wnt via APC cleavage to promote intestinal homeostasis
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10343-8

CASP5 expression is restricted to the human intestinal epithelium, and the CASP5C isoform has a key role in promoting Wnt signalling, which is required for …
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10343-8 CASP5 expression is restricted to the human intestinal epithelium, and the CASP5C isoform has a key role in promoting Wnt signalling, which is required for epithelial homeostasis, through binding to dishevelled and cleavage of APC to regulate β-catenin turnover.

title: Dynamics of genetic and somatic trade-offs in ageing and mortality
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10407-9

Mapping DNA variants that modulate mortality using a starting population of 6,438 young mice defined 29 distinct loci that influence lifespan and mortality …
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10407-9 Mapping DNA variants that modulate mortality using a starting population of 6,438 young mice defined 29 distinct loci that influence lifespan and mortality with divergent age- and sex-specific effects, as well as 30 loci that specifically couple body mass with longevity.

title: Multicentre gene therapy for OTOF-related deafness followed up to 2.5 years

In a multicentre trial of AAV1-hOTOF gene therapy involving 42 participants aged 0.8–32.3 years with autosomal recessive deafness 9, treatment demonstrated …
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10393-y In a multicentre trial of AAV1-hOTOF gene therapy involving 42 participants aged 0.8–32.3 years with autosomal recessive deafness 9, treatment demonstrated safety, tolerability, and improved hearing and speech perception, with age and distortion product otoacoustic emissions associated with treatment outcomes.

title: Myosin forces remodel F-actin for mechanosensitive protein recognition
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10398-7

Structural studies demonstrate that myosin generates forces that cause structural changes in actin, modulating cooperative binding by α-catenin and thus sig…
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10398-7 Structural studies demonstrate that myosin generates forces that cause structural changes in actin, modulating cooperative binding by α-catenin and thus signal transduction and intercellular communication via adhesion complexes.

title: Switchable 2D–3D display through a metasurface lenticular lens
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10318-9

A full-colour 2D–3D switchable light-field display powered by a metasurface lenticular lens is proposed that is a promising solution for next-generation dis…
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10318-9 A full-colour 2D–3D switchable light-field display powered by a metasurface lenticular lens is proposed that is a promising solution for next-generation display technologies in both consumer electronics and commercial applications.

title: Netrin1 blockade alleviates resistance to chemotherapy in pancreatic cancer
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10436-4

The Lap-NET1 phase 1b trial reports that combined netrin1 antibody (NP137) plus modified FOLFIRINOX therapy on patients with locally advanced pancreatic can…
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10436-4 The Lap-NET1 phase 1b trial reports that combined netrin1 antibody (NP137) plus modified FOLFIRINOX therapy on patients with locally advanced pancreatic cancer was well tolerated and improved progression-free survival and overall survival via inhibition of epithelial–mesenchymal transition.

title: Non-equilibrium condensation of the first Solar System solids
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10257-5

A time-dependent model shows chondrite precursors formed by non-equilibrium condensation: cooling rate and pressure alone generate three mineral classes wit…
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10257-5 A time-dependent model shows chondrite precursors formed by non-equilibrium condensation: cooling rate and pressure alone generate three mineral classes with increasing oxidation, matching enstatite, ordinary and carbonaceous chondrites without large redox variations in the solar nebula.

title: A pro-carcinogenic bacterial toxin binds claudin-4 to cleave E-cadherin
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10375-0

CRISPR screening shows that claudin-4 is a receptor for the pro-carcinogenic B. fragilis toxin on colonic epithelial cells, and that this interaction promot…
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10375-0 CRISPR screening shows that claudin-4 is a receptor for the pro-carcinogenic B. fragilis toxin on colonic epithelial cells, and that this interaction promotes cleavage of E-cadherin, leading to epithelial barrier disruption and inflammation.

title: Electronic origin of reorganization energy in interfacial electron transfer
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10311-2

By tuning graphene’s electronic density of states, the study shows electrode electronic structure—not just the electrolyte—dominates reorganization energy a…
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10311-2 By tuning graphene’s electronic density of states, the study shows electrode electronic structure—not just the electrolyte—dominates reorganization energy and thus controls outer-sphere electron-transfer rates at solid–liquid interfaces.

title: Glycerol-driven TNAP activation in thermogenesis and mineralization
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10396-9

Glycerol is an allosteric activator of tissue-nonspecific alkaline phosphatase (TNAP), activating the futile creatine cycle.
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10396-9 Glycerol is an allosteric activator of tissue-nonspecific alkaline phosphatase (TNAP), activating the futile creatine cycle.

title: Chromosomal fusions trigger rediploidization of autopolyploid genomes
summary:

body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10408-8 A printable meta-assembly comprising polystyrene embedded in a polydimethylsiloxane matrix used in roll-to-roll additive nanoprinting is described for the fabrication of highly tunable optical architectures that can span seven orders of magnitude in length.

body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10366-1 Experiments in Arabidopsis demonstrate that group VII ethylene response factors show differential responses to different stimuli, enabling plants in flood-prone environments to adapt to both submergence-induced hypoxia and reoxygenation upon desubmergence.

body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10399-6 Pathologic transformation in KrasG12D-mutant alveolar type II cells initiates a tumour-permissive niche by orchestrating epithelial, stromal and immune reprogramming through amphiregulin–EGFR signalling, creating a self-sustaining circuit critical for lung tumour initiation and progression.

summary:

Analysis of 128 high-coverage Indigenous American genomes shows extensive diversity shaped by several South American dispersals, ancient Australasian admixt…
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10406-w Analysis of 128 high-coverage Indigenous American genomes shows extensive diversity shaped by several South American dispersals, ancient Australasian admixture, archaic introgression and long-term adaptation, indicating a far more complex evolutionary history than previously assumed.

title: ‘Jumping genes’ help a bacterium that causes hospital infections to adapt quickly
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01274-5

Over the past three decades, mobile DNA sequences called transposable elements have proliferated throughout the genome of Enterococcus faecium, an important…
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01274-5 Over the past three decades, mobile DNA sequences called transposable elements have proliferated throughout the genome of Enterococcus faecium, an important bacterium behind infections in hospitalized individuals. These ‘jumping genes’ can replicate rapidly, alter gene activity and seem to help E. faecium to alter its metabolism on clinical timescales.

title: Genomic roots of Indigenous Americans uncovered
summary:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01173-9

A large and diverse collection of Indigenous American genomes uncovers extensive genetic diversity and a complex history of dispersals, continuity and adapt…
body: Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01173-9 A large and diverse collection of Indigenous American genomes uncovers extensive genetic diversity and a complex history of dispersals, continuity and adaptation across the Americas. The findings reveal the evolutionary processes that shaped Indigenous American populations and address the under-representation of these populations in global genomic research.
